_meta.source: Curated genocide & mass-atrocity dataset (human-verified). Per-row academic/institutional citations in data/raw/genocide_curated_verified.csv

sources.curated: Curated genocide & mass-atrocity dataset (human-verified). Per-row academic/institutional citations in data/raw/genocide_curated_verified.csv
sources.ucdp: UCDP One-Sided Violence Dataset v25.1 — Davies et al. (2025), Journal of Peace Research 62(4); Eck & Hultman (2007). https://ucdp.uu.se/downloads/

_meta.rummel_note: Rummel 20th-c democide considered but NOT used: summary tables are GIF images (not machine-readable); transcribing would violate the no-fabrication rule.
_meta.license: Compiled from published academic sources; cite individual rows.
_meta.death_basis: curated: deaths_mid (scholarly midpoint of cited range); ucdp: sum of yearly best fatality estimates
_meta.self_arcs: role_from == role_to country (state killing its own population)

arcs.source: Yang Jisheng. Tombstone (2008/2012); Dikötter. Mao's Great Famine. Bloomsbury, 2010.
Rudnytskyi et al. (2015) Demography of a man-made catastrophe. Canadian Studies in Population 42(1-2).
arcs.notes: Soviet-imposed famine. 3.9M excess deaths + 0.6M lost births. Recognized as genocide by 30+ countries.

arcs.source: Hilberg, Raul. The Destruction of the European Jews. Yale University Press, 3rd ed.
Kiernan, Ben. The Pol Pot Regime. Yale UP, 2004.
arcs.notes: 15-36% of 1975 population killed. ECCC tribunal convictions.

arcs.source: Sanderson, J-P. (2020) Demographic analysis of the Congo Free State. Population.
arcs.notes: Sanderson most likely: 1.2M. Hochschild 10M rejected by demographers.

arcs.source: Bloxham, Donald. The Great Game of Genocide. Oxford UP, 2005.
arcs.notes: Ottoman Armenians. Widely recognized as genocide.

arcs.source: Bharadwaj, Khwaja & Mian (2008). Harvard scholars (2018): 2.3-3.2M.
arcs.notes: Communal violence during British withdrawal. Traditional: 200K-2M. Recent: up to 3.4M.

arcs.source: Bharadwaj, Khwaja & Mian (2008). Harvard scholars (2018): 2.3-3.2M.
arcs.notes: Same event — violence affected both sides equally.

arcs.source: Hilberg, Raul. The Destruction of the European Jews. Yale University Press, 3rd ed.
arcs.notes: Ukrainian Jews — Babi Yar and Einsatzgruppen.

arcs.source: Richmond, Walter. The Circassian Genocide. UBC Press, 2013.
arcs.notes: North Caucasus. Russian Empire expelled/killed majority of Circassian population.

arcs.source: UN official estimate (1994): 800K. Rwandan Govt census: 1,074,017.
USHMM Holocaust Encyclopedia; Hilberg 3rd ed.
Declassified Soviet NKVD archives (1992); Sciences Po Mass Violence.
arcs.notes: NKVD Order #00447 executions. 681K documented from archives.

arcs.source: USHMM Holocaust Encyclopedia; Yad Vashem.
arcs.notes: Hungarian Jews — mass deportations to Auschwitz 1944.

arcs.source: PLOS ONE (2025): Death toll among the Bangladeshi refugees of the 1971 war.
arcs.notes: Range reflects major scholarly disagreement. Bangladesh govt claims 3M.

arcs.source: Jones, Adam. Genocide: A Comprehensive Introduction. Routledge, 2006.
arcs.notes: Anatolian/Pontic Greeks. Range reflects scholarly debate.

arcs.source: Cribb, Robert. How many deaths? in The Indonesian Killings of 1965-66. Monash.
Hancock, Ian. We Are the Romani People. U of Hertfordshire, 2002; USHMM.
arcs.notes: Roma/Sinti across Nazi-occupied Europe. Range reflects scholarly uncertainty.

arcs.source: Brundige et al., 'Indonesian Human Rights Abuses in West Papua', Allard K. Lowenstein Intl Human Rights Clinic, Yale Law School (2004); Anderson, Kjell, 'A Slow-Motion Genocide: Indonesian Rule in West Papua', Griffith Journal of Law & Human Dignity (2015); Genocide Watch country report.
arcs.notes: Toll is CONTESTED and lacks a rigorous demographic study (unlike CAVR for East Timor). Advocacy and some academic sources cite 'up to 500,000' Papuan deaths since 1963; the low end is more conservative. Included as a flagged, low-confidence entry.

arcs.source: Hilberg 3rd ed.; USHMM.
arcs.notes: Romanian Jews — Iași pogrom, Transnistria deportations.

arcs.source: Gaunt, David. Massacres, Resistance, Protectors. Gorgias Press, 2006.
USHMM Holocaust Encyclopedia.
Hilberg 3rd ed.; USHMM.
IMTFE (Tokyo Trial): 155,300 burial records. Chinese govt memorial: 300K.
arcs.notes: 6-week massacre. Range reflects Japan-China scholarly disagreement.

arcs.source: Commission for Reception, Truth and Reconciliation in East Timor (CAVR), 'Chega!' Report (2005); Human Rights Data Analysis Group (HRDAG).
arcs.notes: CAVR/HRDAG: minimum 102,800 (+/-11,000) conflict-related deaths 1974-1999 — ~18,600 killings + ~84,200 deaths from imposed hunger and illness; possibly up to 200,000. Indonesian invasion Dec 1975 to 1999.

arcs.source: Hilberg 3rd ed.; USHMM.
USHMM Holocaust Encyclopedia.
arcs.notes: Dutch Jews — 75% of Jewish population killed.

arcs.source: Lancet Global Health (Jan 2025): 75,200 violent + 8,540 excess deaths.
arcs.notes: Ongoing. ICJ ruled 'plausible' genocide (Jan 2024). ICC arrest warrants issued. Final ruling pending.

arcs.source: Olusoga & Erichsen. The Kaiser's Holocaust. Faber & Faber, 2010.
arcs.notes: Herero: 40-80K (80% of pop). Nama: 10K (50% of pop). Germany recognized 2021.

arcs.source: USHMM Holocaust Encyclopedia.
USHMM Holocaust Encyclopedia.
USHMM Holocaust Encyclopedia.
USHMM Holocaust Encyclopedia.
USHMM Holocaust Encyclopedia.
arcs.notes: Greek Jews — Thessaloniki community nearly destroyed.

arcs.source: Evans & Ørsted-Jensen (2014); 'Speculating about genocide: the Queensland frontier 1859–1897', Australian Economic History Review (doi:10.1111/aehr.12278); Reynolds, Henry, The Other Side of the Frontier (1981); Univ. of Newcastle Colonial Frontier Massacres map.
arcs.notes: Highly contested; no single agreed national figure. Reynolds: >=20,000 Aboriginal deaths nationally 1788-1900 (+ up to 3,000 settlers). Evans & Ørsted-Jensen: >=65,180 killed in QUEENSLAND ALONE 1820s-1900s (66,680 incl. settlers), implying a higher national toll.

arcs.source: USHMM Holocaust Encyclopedia.
Madley, Benjamin. An American Genocide: The United States and the California Indian Catastrophe, 1846-1873. Yale University Press, 2016.
arcs.notes: Madley documents 9,492-16,094 direct killings by vigilantes, state militia and US Army; mid = midpoint of his range. California Indian population fell from ~150,000 to ~30,000 in the period.

arcs.source: MSF surveys (Nov 2017): 6,700 killed in first month. UN Independent Fact-Finding Mission.
arcs.notes: Myanmar military 'clearance operations'. 650K+ displaced. ICJ genocide case ongoing.

arcs.source: USHMM Holocaust Encyclopedia.
ICTY Trial Chamber, Prosecutor v. Krstić (2001). Appeals Chamber confirmed genocide (2004).
arcs.notes: Bosniak men and boys. ICTY-confirmed genocide.

arcs.source: Truth and Reconciliation Commission of South Africa Report (1998).
arcs.notes: TRC: 7K political deaths (1948-89). 21K figure = testimonies received, not confirmed deaths. Corrected.

arcs.source: UN Commission of Inquiry on Syria (2016). OHCHR (2024).
arcs.notes: ISIS targeting of Yazidi community. 10,800 women/girls trafficked. UN-confirmed genocide.

arcs.source: Thornton, Russell. 'Cherokee Population Losses During the Trail of Tears.' Ethnohistory 31(4), 1984; American Indian Holocaust and Survival, U. Oklahoma Press, 1987.
arcs.notes: Death estimates range 2,000-8,000; ~4,000 is the commonly cited figure (Mooney 1900). Forced removal of the Cherokee Nation under the 1830 Indian Removal Act.

arcs.source: Truth and Reconciliation Commission of Canada, Final Report (2015), Vol. 4: Missing Children and Unmarked Burials.
arcs.notes: TRC confirmed 3,201 student deaths; TRC Chair Murray Sinclair estimated the true toll likely >6,000 (high). TRC wording: 'cultural genocide'; Canadian House of Commons recognized it as genocide (Oct 2022).

arcs.source: USHMM Holocaust Encyclopedia.
USHMM Holocaust Encyclopedia.
Hilberg 3rd ed.
arcs.notes: ~250K survivors immigrated to Israel 1948-51. Deaths=0 (counted in origin countries). Included for intergenerational trauma transmission.
